facts.text: North Hills earned its name because the village sits in the northern part of Nassau County and the terrain is noticeably hilly.
The glacial action that formed much of Long Island deposited Shelter Rock in what is now North Hills about 11,000 years ago.
If you need a train, the nearest Long Island Rail Road stops are Manhasset on the Port Washington Branch and Roslyn on the Oyster Bay Branch.
During the 1960s through the 1980s, North Hills proposed a controversial master plan that would have allowed buildings up to 85 feet tall, sparking a heated debate.
In 1906, William Singer of the Singer Sewing Machine Company built a federal‑style estate in North Hills, adding a touch of industrial heritage to the village.
On May 16, 1983, North Hills dedicated Lowell H., a park that the village owns and operates, honoring a local benefactor.
